description: Cross-language conformance for a BUILD-ONCE SPATIAL FIELD materialized once at setup and consumed elementwise by an ODE (CONFORMANCE_SPEC.md §5.12). A geometry leaf (`polygon_intersection_area` over const cell rings) produces a per-cell `area` field; a build-once centered first difference of it — authored as the periodic `makearray` STENCIL a discretization rule lowers `D` to — yields `darea`; and a per-cell ODE `D(u[c]) = darea[c] - u[c]` GATHERS the build-once `darea` at every RHS call. This locks the two paths the terrain-regrid coupling surfaced in the Julia runner: (1) the setup-time materializer must evaluate a NON-aggregate build-once array op (a `makearray` with no `output_idx`, plus `reshape`) — not only aggregates; and (2) a build-once materialized array must be exposed to the ODE RHS as a gatherable const array (`index(darea, c)`). The forcing is CONST, so the golden trajectory u_c(t) = darea_c (1 - e^-t) is analytic and exact. Complements `tests/conformance/geometry/` (build-once regrid areas, no downstream ODE) and `tests/conformance/subsystem_loader/` (bind a CONST loader field consumed bare/gather, no geometry).
